{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"At High Tea in the Rehab. Hospital,
Miss Paragreen, stone deaf, lets
forth a body noise like a starter's gun,","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"does not pause in spooning yellow jelly,
nor do the other three, but Mrs Clausing
and myself, dash into sentences loudly...","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Saturday evening. Behind the servery counter,
above the chatter and clatter of crockery,
beyond the hearing of most silvery heads","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"a radio pops and crackles. Kindly nurses
shout; outside sun shadows the tee-tree fence
and clouds whiz inland, shapeless as these days.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"Then through it all there is a tune, a "golden oldie"
which echoed down those Devon summer days
when I was obsessed, possessed by a postgrad girl:","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"bangled, long-skirted, she wore patouli oil,
her long hair was an auburn tent, her deep voice
played as plangent as a cello in my chest.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"},{"data":{},"content":[{"data":{},"marks":[],"value":"My bald-headed gallahadery made her smile...
Fifty now, if she's alive, and I'm among the wrinklies,
glad only that I am not next to Miss Paragreen.","nodeType":"text"}],"nodeType":"paragraph"}],"nodeType":"document"}
